content:
Planning retirement income is the most important financial task for anyone in their 40s. PPF or LIC pension - the honest answer is they serve different purposes and combining both is often optimal.

Public Provident Fund: Current rate 7.1% per annum (government-set). 15-year lock-in with partial withdrawal from year 7. Fully EEE tax status - exempt at investment (Section 80C), exempt on interest, exempt on maturity. Maximum annual contribution Rs 1.5 lakh. Zero risk.

LIC Pension Plans such as Jeevan Akshay VII and New Jeevan Shanti: Immediate or deferred annuity plans. Invest a lump sum at retirement, LIC pays fixed monthly income for life. Annuity rate for a 60-year-old today: approximately 6 to 6.5% on a joint-life basis. The annuity is taxable as income.

They work in sequence: build with PPF, convert with pension. Real numbers for a 40-year-old contributing Rs 1.5 lakh/year to PPF for 20 years at 7.1%: approximately Rs 65 lakh by age 60. Investing Rs 65 lakh in LIC Jeevan Akshay at 6.2% gives approximately Rs 33,800/month for life for both spouses.

My recommendation: Max your PPF every year for Section 80C saving plus guaranteed returns. At retirement, use LIC pension plan to convert part of the corpus into guaranteed income. Use the remaining corpus in balanced mutual funds for inflation protection.

Call me at 9415313434 for a personalised retirement income projection.

Use our free [Retirement Calculator](/calculators/retirement) to calculate your own numbers.
contentHi:
PPF: 7.1%, पूर्ण EEE टैक्स छूट, Rs 1.5 लाख/वर्ष, 15 वर्ष लॉक-इन।

LIC पेंशन (जीवन अक्षय VII): एकमुश्त जमा, आजीवन मासिक आय। 60 वर्ष में ~6-6.5%/वर्ष।

वास्तविक गणना: 40 वर्षीय Rs 1.5 लाख/वर्ष PPF 20 वर्ष = Rs 65 लाख। Rs 65 लाख LIC जीवन अक्षय में = Rs 33,800/माह आजीवन।

सलाह: PPF से बनाएं, LIC पेंशन से नियमित आय लें, बाकी बैलेंस्ड MF में। 9415313434 पर कॉल करें।
